heading.zh: 操作词替代大多数动词

body.en: Basic English uses only ~18 operator-verbs (come, get, give, go, keep, let, make, put, take, be, do, have, say, see, send, may, will). Combine an operator with a directive to replace thousands of verbs: go in = enter, get up = rise, put on = wear, take away = remove.
body.zh: 基础英语只用约18个操作动词（come, get, give, go, keep, let, make, put, take, be, do, have, say, see, send, may, will）。把操作词与方位词组合，就能替代成千上万的动词：go in=进入，get up=起身，put on=穿上，take away=拿走。

gloss.en: to move toward a person or place

words.example: Come here and sit with me.
I got a call from Dave this morning.
Give the letter to your mother.

gloss.en: to move from one place to another

words.example: Let's go to the park now.

gloss.en: to allow somebody to do something

words.example: Let me help you with that.
She will make a cake for us.

gloss.en: to move something into a place

words.example: Put the cases down there, please.

gloss.en: to give the impression of being something
to carry or move something to another place

words.example: Remember to take your coat when you leave.

gloss.en: to exist or to have a quality

words.example: What are you doing this evening?
He had a new car and a boat.

gloss.en: to speak words; to tell something

words.example: Please say your name out loud.

gloss.en: to notice something with your eyes

words.example: She could not see him in the crowd.

gloss.en: to make something go to a place

words.example: She sent a letter of support.

gloss.en: used to show that something is possible
used to talk about the future
